channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Israel's growing fight over Palestinian symbols
item.description: For some it's a symbol of identity. For others, a challenge to the state. NPR's Itay Stern reports on the debate over the Palestinian flag in Israel.
item.content:encoded: For some it's a symbol of identity. For others, a challenge to the state. NPR's Itay Stern reports on the debate over the Palestinian flag in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Itay Stern

item.title: For Pakistan's Ismaili muslims, a visit from their spiritual leader brings a homecoming
item.description: For many Ismaili Muslims, seeing the Aga Khan is a once-in-a-lifetime event. NPR's Betsy Joles reports from his visit to remote northern Pakistan.
item.content:encoded: For many Ismaili Muslims, seeing the Aga Khan is a once-in-a-lifetime event. NPR's Betsy Joles reports from his visit to remote northern Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: Iran claims Hormuz closure, U.S. says ships still passing
item.description: Iran says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again. The U.S. military says traffic is still flowing. NPR's Jane Arraf reports from Beirut.
item.content:encoded: Iran says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again. The U.S. military says traffic is still flowing. NPR's Jane Arraf reports from Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: These nuns spent a lifetime helping others. In their last years, who will help them?
item.description: The sisters of Uganda are teachers, health-care advocates and more. Those who are in their twilight of their life need help. Who will come to their aid?
item.content:encoded:
The sisters of Uganda are teachers, health-care advocates and more. Those who are in their twilight of their life need help. Who will come to their aid?

(Image credit: Stuart Tibaweswa for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sophie Neiman

item.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz shut as U.S.-Iran talks set for Sunday in Switzerland
item.description: U.S. and Iranian teams are set to hold "technical-level" talks, according to Pakistan's foreign ministry. But fighting in Lebanon and claims over the Strait of Hormuz threaten a tentative agreement.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. and Iranian teams are set to hold "technical-level" talks, according to Pakistan's foreign ministry. But fighting in Lebanon and claims over the Strait of Hormuz threaten a tentative agreement.

(Image credit: Hassan Ammar)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Italy's Meloni, once Trump's closest ally in Europe, says he made up a story about her
item.description: "Italy and I do not beg," Meloni said in a video rebuke posted on social media Friday. Italy's top diplomat, meanwhile, said he was cancelling a visit to the U.S because of the alleged remarks.
item.content:encoded:
"Italy and I do not beg," Meloni said in a video rebuke posted on social media Friday. Italy's top diplomat, meanwhile, said he was cancelling a visit to the U.S because of the alleged remarks.

(Image credit: Geert Vanden Wijngaert)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Rosman

item.title: US-Iran talks in Switzerland canceled. And, DHS to give police facial recognition app
item.description: The U.S.-Iran talks that were set to happen in Switzerland have been canceled. And, the Department of Homeland Security has plans to give some local police access to ICE facial recognition technology.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S.-Iran talks that were set to happen in Switzerland have been canceled. And, the Department of Homeland Security has plans to give some local police access to ICE facial recognition technology.

(Image credit: Ken Cedeno)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: What you need to know about the preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement signed by Trump
item.description: Here's a look at the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran, and the challenges that remain to find lasting peace.
item.content:encoded:
Here's a look at the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran, and the challenges that remain to find lasting peace.

(Image credit: Iranian Presidency Office via AP)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Do Middle East nations still see the U.S. as a reliable ally?
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Dania Thafer of the Gulf International Forum whether countries in the Middle East consider the U.S. a reliable ally.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Dania Thafer of the Gulf International Forum whether countries in the Middle East consider the U.S. a reliable ally.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Labour's Andy Burnham wins a special election, setting up a showdown with Starmer to lead Britain
item.description: Labour's Andy Burnham, the current mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a special election for a seat in Parliament that puts him in a position to challenge embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer for leadership of the country.
item.content:encoded:
Labour's Andy Burnham, the current mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a special election for a seat in Parliament that puts him in a position to challenge embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer for leadership of the country.

(Image credit: Jon Super)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
